leads.description: AI-powered design search and Mobbin alternative.
Running app with Whoop integration. Zero to 5K training in 10 weeks with recovery-based advice.
top_gaps: Zero social proof — founder admitted getting only 2 signups in 6 years across 5 products
No clear CTA — page lists features but no 'Start Training' or 'Connect Whoop' button visible
Positioning is feature-focused not benefit-focused — 'GPS tracking, 5 heart rate zones' vs 'Actually finish your first 5K'
leads.outreach_draft:
Hey! Just saw your Runphatic post on r/indiehackers — congrats on the first 2 signups after 6 years! The Whoop integration for recovery-based training advice is genuinely clever.

Quick feedback: your landing page lists features but doesn't have an obvious CTA. Where do I click to start? Also, leading with 'Zero to 5K in 10 weeks' as the headline (benefit) vs feature specs would help conversion.

Crowdstake is an AI marketing OS that generates landing pages with benefit-focused copy — could help you nail that first impression. Free tier at crowdstake.ai.

leads.description: Open-source macOS launcher alternative to Raycast. AI prompt, clipboard, notes, voice typing, extensions.
top_gaps: 1k GitHub stars mentioned on Reddit but not prominently displayed on landing page
No testimonials from actual users — just feature descriptions
Free/open-source positioning may limit monetization potential
leads.outreach_draft:
